title: DefinedTerm
description: A word, name, acronym, phrase, etc. with a formal definition.
$comment:
This is an implementation of schema.org
[`DefinedTerm`](https://schema.org/DefinedTerm).

In Stencila Schema it is used for terms with explicit definitions or controlled
meanings, such as glossary entries, classification terms, subject headings,
and named conceptual categories.

Key properties include `name`, `termCode`, and the inherited `description`
field, which is typically used for the term's definition.

analogues.notes: Approximate JATS analogue for marked terms, though JATS `<term>` is inline and does not by itself model a standalone defined-term entity.

type.$comment: This is a special property analogous to JSON-LD's `@type` keyword.

id.$comment: This is a special property analogous to JSON-LD's `@id` keyword.

identifiers.description: Any kind of identifier for any kind of Thing.
identifiers.$comment:
Some identifiers have specific properties e.g the `issn` property for
the `Periodical` type. These should be used in preference to this
property which is intended for identifiers that do not yet have a
specific property. Identifiers can be represented as strings, but
using a `PropertyValue` will usually be better because it allows
for `propertyID` (i.e. the type of identifier).

termCode.description: A code that identifies this DefinedTerm within a DefinedTermSet
